feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Everyone talks about postpartum depression — beware these other mental health issues after childbirth
entry.summary: Maternal mental health conditions are the leading cause of preventable maternal mortality and morbidity in the US.

entry.title: Men's health slumps in midlife – but these simple lifestyle changes will add years to your life
entry.summary: The stereotype says men ignore their health because they’re careless, but doctors don’t agree

entry.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo outbreak top 1,000 with 254 deaths, authorities say
entry.summary: Authorities say confirmed cases of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo have reached 1,003, including 254 deaths

entry.title: Man taken into custody after multi-county police chase
entry.summary: A man was taken into custody after leading police on a multi-county chase Sunday afternoon.The police pusuit started in Porter County

entry.title: How To Keep Ticks Out Of Your Yard This Summer
entry.summary: Make your yard a tick-free zone this summer by mowing the lawn, trimming shrubs, and getting rid of lawn debris. Plus, learn how to protect your skin outdoors.

entry.title: 4 Supplements That Can Damage Your Liver
entry.summary: Some popular supplements, like green tea extract and turmeric, have been linked to liver injury. Here's what to know and how to choose safely.

entry.title: GLP-1 drug tirzepatide may boost calorie-burning brown fat
entry.summary: New research suggests GLP-1 drug tirzepatide may increase brown fat activity, helping the body burn more calories beyond reducing appetite.

entry.title: Is hearing loss an Alzheimer’s warning sign? Research shows new risk factors
entry.summary: The number of people living with Alzheimer's disease is expected to double by 2060. New research is shedding light on warning signs and risk factors that could signal cognitive decline ahead.

entry.title: Dementia Cases Climb, but We Aren't Powerless Against It
entry.summary: Bryan Walsh makes the argument in a piece for Vox

entry.title: These Lifestyle Changes Help Lower Your Risk of Chronic Disease For Decades
entry.summary: The Diabetes Prevention Program (DPP) and its long-running follow-up, the Diabetes Prevention Program Outcomes Study (DPPOS), have tracked thousands of people for more than two decades, examining how lifestyle changes can ultimately influence health.

entry.title: River Cleanup Employee Blames Cancer on the Work
entry.summary: Milwaukee Journal Sentinel explores the case related to the Fox River

entry.title: Research Suggests the Older You Get, the More Weed You Should Smoke
entry.summary: Research suggests that cannabis helps protect the aging brain against biological wear and tear and neurodegenerative diseases.

entry.title: For Millions, Ordinary Sounds Turn Into Excruciating Ordeals
entry.summary: Researchers push for official WHO code for misophonia as patients seek validation, treatment

entry.title: Is hearing loss an Alzheimer’s warning sign? Research shows new risk factors
entry.summary: The number of people living with Alzheimer's disease is expected to double by 2060. New research is shedding light on warning signs and risk factors that could signal cognitive decline ahead.

entry.title: Netizens spiral over IIT Roorkee’s cow urine study, findings reveal potential to fight Chikungunya virus
entry.summary: Cow Urine Research: IIT Roorkee finds cow urine distillate has bioactive compounds reducing Chikungunya viral load. The revelation stirs online debate, challenging scientific credibility and sparking discussions on merging traditional knowledge and biotechnol…

entry.title: Chewing sugary gum after eating certain veggies is good for your heart: study
entry.summary: New research shows that popping a piece after a meal could boost the benefits of certain vegetables, which is especially good news for heart health.

entry.title: ‘Albert Sabin’ Review: A Disease’s Lifetime Enemy
entry.summary: Albert Sabin concluded that the global elimination of polio required an oral vaccine comprising weakened, live viruses.

entry.title: 3 Studies Link Diet, Fitness, Multivitamins to Slower Aging
entry.summary: Recent research has explored the possible effects of diet, daily multivitamins, and cardiorespiratory fitness on aging. Learn about the findings here.

entry.title: Dietician reveals the 'most dangerous time of the day' for diabetics and shares 5 simple ways to manage it | Health
entry.summary: Waking up to high blood sugar despite not eating? Nutritionist Shweta Panchal explains the Dawn Phenomenon and shares simple ways to manage it. | Health

entry.title: Early-onset cancers are on the rise. Knowing your family history is crucial.
entry.summary: Researchers have not been able to find a single cause for the rise in early-onset cancers. Instead, research suggests a myriad of factors play a role, including obesity, heavy alcohol use…

entry.title: How fast you should be able to walk a mile at every age — and what it can say about your longevity
entry.summary: It’s not just how far you walk, as pace can also reveal important information about health and longevity.

entry.title: “Wow, that’s a lot”: Woman Pulls The Ultimate Power Move On Her Co-Worker After Years of Lunchroom Harassment
entry.summary: A woman on Reddit posted a picture of a burrito “bigger than my forearm” that she wholeheartedly enjoyed at her desk. She described it as her response to a c...

entry.title: After Opening Up About Cancer, A Man Feels Hurt When His Friend Shifts The Focus To Herself
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Clues to psilocybin's epigenetic effects on people with alcohol use disorder
entry.summary: Some psychedelics, psychoactive substances that alter people's mood, perceptions and mental processes, were recently found to be promising alternative treatments for some mental health disorders. The substance that has attracted the most interest so far is ps…

entry.title: Tubulin prevents toxic brain protein clumps linked to Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s
entry.summary: Scientists at Baylor College of Medicine may have uncovered a promising new way to combat Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s disease. Instead of trying to stop Tau and alpha-synuclein proteins from gathering into tiny droplets inside brain cells, the researchers fou…

entry.title: 3 Weekend Habits That Are Quietly Increasing Your Diabetes Risk
entry.summary: Your weekend habits can play a big role in developing type 2 diabetes. Diabetes experts share common weekend habits that can increase diabetes risk over time.

entry.title: 6 Canned Foods to Eat to Lower Blood Pressure, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Canned foods are less expensive than fresh produce and can help you eat more fruits and veggies, which can help lower blood pressure.

entry.title: Scientists Say This 1 Vitamin May Protect Your Brain as You Age
entry.summary: A new study found that older adults with higher vitamin C levels were more likely to have more gray matter and stronger brain connectivity.

entry.title: Did Dementia Turn Small-Town Doctor Into a Predator?
entry.summary: New Yorker examines the troubling case of Ray Howell in Indiana

entry.title: Can Sparkling Water Increase Your Risk Of Colorectal Cancer?
entry.summary: If you sip on canned sparkling water or seltzer regularly, listen up.

entry.title: No more painful biopsies? How a new blood test will transform cancer detection
entry.summary: New liquid biopsies aim to deliver simpler, more accurate cancer detection by analysing DNA fragment size in blood samples.

entry.title: Trust in medical researchers has tanked. Gillette thinks paying subjects might help.
entry.summary: Gillette Children’s in St. Paul follows an uncommon practice of paying regular people and former patients to help shape clinical trials and pick research targets.

entry.title: Second person suspected of Ebola put in isolation, health ministry says
entry.summary: The person is the second individual to have returned from the Democratic Republic of the Congo recently, suffering from symptoms that might indicate the contraction of the deadly virus. The two were put in isolation, and test results are expected in the comin…

entry.title: What to know about the temporary Medicare GLP-1 Bridge program
entry.summary: Starting in July, some Medicare beneficiaries will be able to access GLP-1 medications by paying one flat fee per month. The temporary program is set to run for a year-and-a-half through the end of 2027. But with less than two weeks before its launch, questio…

entry.title: A doctor explains how to boost your longevity in just 4 minutes a day, with exercises you can do at home
entry.summary: Exercise is key to healthy aging, and 30-second sessions of squats and push-ups may be enough to build strength and help you live longer, a doctor said.

entry.title: Healthy ageing doesn’t begin at 60: Doctor shares daily habits that can decide how well you’ll live later
entry.summary: Ageing has a curious way of surprising people. Many spend their younger years chasing deadlines, neglecting sleep, skipping exercise, and assuming there will always be time to take care of health later. Then one day, aching knees, rising blood pressure, poor …

entry.title: A Family Dispute Breaks Out After A Man Becomes Ill From Food Prepared By His Sister And Refuses To Eat There Again
entry.summary: The best of the internet, sifted, and sorted.

entry.title: Health Division worried by high rates of STIs among young people in Tobago
entry.summary: The Di­vi­sion of Health is ex­press­ing alarm over “high rates” of sex­u­al­ly trans­mit­ted in­fec­tions (STIs) in To­ba­go, par­tic­u­lar­ly HIV, among the 20-29 age group.

entry.title: An 83-year-old trainer’s strength training formula for the over-65s
entry.summary: If you’re over 65 and want to start strength training, try this workout plan

entry.title: ‘Only death can stop me’: dying Chinese tycoon works 12 hours daily to cure ALS
entry.summary: Top e-commerce executive who has survived several near-death episodes, says ‘faith’ keeps him going in the ��darkest night’.

entry.title: H5N1 bird flu confirmed in Australia for the first time, meaning virus has now reached every continent
entry.summary: Australia confirms its first case of H5N1 bird flu in a seabird near Esperance, Western Australia, meaning the virus has now reached every continent.

entry.title: Frozen fruit and canned veg are cheap, but are they as healthy as fresh food?
entry.summary: You probably know fruit and vegetables are good for you, and most of us don't eat enough of them.

entry.title: 3 Common Men's Health Drugs Have Surprising Origins
entry.summary: Many medicines begin life with one purpose and end up proving useful for a completely different reason.

entry.title: Developing story: Crash closes Taft Bridge on Connecticut Avenue NW
entry.summary: A Park Police chase ends with a moped rider getting hit and killed on the Taft Bridge in Northwest D.C.

entry.title: 7-Day High-Fiber Meal Plan to Lower Triglycerides, Created by a Dietitian
entry.summary: A high fiber intake is linked to improved triglyceride levels. Follow this high-fiber meal plan created by a dietitian to support a healthy heart.

entry.title: Measles case confirmed in Maryland resident who passed through Dulles and DC clinic
entry.summary: A confirmed measles case has been found in a Maryland resident who traveled through Dulles Airport and went to an urgent care office in the District.

entry.title: 6 Foods To Help Lower Blood Pressure Besides Pomegranate Juice
entry.summary: Pomegranate juice is known to help with hypertension, a condition affecting about half of adults in the U.S. These everyday foods can help too.

entry.title: The 5 Best Cereals to Reduce Stroke Risk, According to Cardiologists and Dietitians
entry.summary: Your breakfast bowl can be a powerful step toward better heart health.

entry.title: The hobby that can rewire your brain and help you build resilience
entry.summary: Experts say resilience may be as ordinary as the journal entries people scribble, the emails they exchange, the task lists they create

entry.title: 6 of the Best Foods for Protein and Fiber, According to Nutritionists
entry.summary: High-protein high-fiber foods are filling, low in fat, and high in essential nutrients. We asked nutrition experts to share which are the best foods for both protein and fiber and these are the ones they chose.
